observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refund the amount of ₱1.20 million representing payment for cash assistance to BHERT members and other LGU personnel which was not in accordance with the purpose of BGCM Fund and thus considered as irregular expenditure
observations.reason: The LGU practiced substance over form since there were restrictions imposed during pandemic. The intention of granting food allowance in the form of cash assistance is to help our frontliners buy the basic commodities they need such as food so they could execute properly their tasks to our people. This is the best option to take during that time due to restrictions such as difficulty in transportation and refusal of caterer to render services. Our frontliners play a vital role in implementing LGU’s COVID related programs so we should also give important to what they need to properly execute their tasks.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the agency to strictly observe the provision under COA Circular 2007-001 particularly Section 4.5 and 5.0 on the grant and utilization of financial assistance.  We also recommended that all NGOs/POs who were granted financial assistance shall be required to submit liquidation report for proper evaluation, monitoring and recording of the transaction in the books of accounts.

Accounting entries shall be made to set up the “Due from NGOs/POs” account.  Expense shall only be recognized upon submission of Liquidation Report together with the supporting documents as evidence of disbursements.

We recommended also to the agency to create a committee that will accredit NGOs/POs soliciting financial assistance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the MA to prepare and submit regularly/ monthly BRS with supporting documents for the 13 bank accounts maintained by the LGU, in compliance with Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011.
We also recommended that the LCE require the MA to submit supporting documents to justify the reconciling items and the unreconciled amount between the bank and books in the BRS submitted by the MA with bank account no. 4332-1003-49.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare JEVs for the necessary correcting/adjusting entries and to record the same to bring the affected accounts to corrected balances.
We also recommended strict compliance with Section 112 of PD No. 1445, Paragraph 15 of PAS 1 and COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the procurement of food, meal for COVID-19 patients and other supplies shall be done by way of public bidding, or any alternative mode of procurement as provided by Section 10 & 12 of R.A. 9184.  All transactions pertaining to procurement should be evaluated by the Bids and Awards Committee, and a corresponding resolution thereto should be prepared and attached to the corresponding disbursement voucher.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct all Departments and End-users to refrain from procuring through reimbursements & to pay directly by issuing check to the suppliers.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
review the aforementioned stale checks, secure a copy of the original Disbursement Voucher (DV) and thereafter prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) following the procedures prescribed on NGAS for LGUs to ensure recording of such cancellation; and
facilitate the submission to the COA of the aforecited cancelled checks and RCI prepared in accordance with the requirements of the NGAS Manual for validation.
We also recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer:
notify the creditors of the LGU to immediately file the request for replacement of identified stale checks.  Henceforth, the AMT shall also send written notification to payees at least 1 month before the check becomes stale; and
prepare the monthly RCI, ensures that all cancelled checks are attached and reported chronologically with the other checks issued with the word “Cancelled” clearly indicated therein.

We recommended that the AMT:
instruct all Collecting Officers to prepare their individual Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) as supporting reports to the CRAAF.
submit the monthly CRAAF accompanied by the RAAF per AO to the Audit Team, through the Accounting Office not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month as provided under the NGAS manual.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to record the replenishment of the petty cash fund under the imprest system, as illustrated in the following entries;
We also recommended that Management require the Petty Cash Custodian to prepare Disbursement Voucher (duly supported by a list/summary of PCVs and its supporting documents) for the replenishment when the expenses incurred reach at least 75% of the PCF established.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct:
the Inventory Committee to exclude the properties from RPCPPE with costs below the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00 each.
the Municipal Accountant to identify those properties with a cost below P15,000 each which erroneously recorded as in the PPE Accounts and prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify these items or drop from the account as PPE.
the General Services Officer to issue the corresponding Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) for items below the capitalization threshold but with useful life of more than one year.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and GSO to maintain subsidiary ledgers/PPELC and property cards for each category of property, plant & equipment account as required by Section 114 of NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials and employees to submit the required documents and that Management ensure completeness of documentary requirements necessary to substantiate the claims before processing.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) strictly enforce compliance with the requirements set forth in the provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to record the grant of financial assistance to NGOs under the Due from NGOs/POs Account and require the liquidation thereof.

We recommended that Management implement the remaining development projects. Should the PPAs considered to be no longer viable and essential, LGU may realign to other priority projects needed. Also, strictly observe the guidelines set in DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2017-1 and DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the:
Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with the LDRRM Officer to restore back to the LDRRMF from the General Fund Proper the amount of ₱237,320.00 to make the funds available for future use. Corresponding correction to the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) must also be made to adjust the amount of expenditures under the General Fund Proper and the LDRRMF;
Municipal Budget Officer to observe the proper charging of expenditures specifically the LDRRMF as prescribed by the aforementioned guidelines; and
MDRRMC of strict compliance with Item 4.3 of the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-01

We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC to ensure compliance with posting requirements for procurement under emergency cases/alternative methods, pursuant to Section 54.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to observe the disclosure of the ABC in the RFQ and adhere strictly with Section 31.1 of Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bid & Award Committee (BAC) to see to it that all the documents required by Section 9.1.3.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, are compliant before declaring the lowest calculated bidder as responsive.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall ensure that the supporting documents are complete before processing the payment for the procured medicines/drugs, in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to deduct/withheld retention money or require special bank guarantee before processing the payment. Adhere strictly with the provision prescribed in Section 62.1 of the Revised IRR-RA 9184.

We recommended that the Management require the General Services Office to submit PO/letter order/contract to the AT within five days from the issuance thereof in adherence with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Management require the General Services Office or the concerned office preparing the PO to provide complete data/information on the letter order/contract as required per COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant not to process disbursement for payment of AICS without supporting documents required by the LGU Manual Operation. Comply with the guidelines and documentary requirements prescribed by their own Manual of Operations. Likewise, adhere strictly with Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
We recommended that the Management instruct the Focal Person to submit GAD Reports within the period prescribed by Section 5 of COA Circular 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

observations.reason: Management did not demand the concerned debtors to pay their loans.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee to take charge the annual physical count of PPE and prepare the required RPCPPE for timely submission to the Office of the Auditor.
The Accounting Office and General Services Office/Treasurer’s Office maintain and reconcile the required PPE ledger cards and property cards, and match the balances of PPE accounts per books with the RPCPPE.

We recommended the following:
The OIC – Municipal Treasurer cancel the unclaimed stale checks and return the original Disbursement Vouchers to the Municipal Accountant in order to effect the necessary adjusting entries.  For the checks which became stale in the hand of payee, send written notice reminding them of the need to present their stale checks for cancellation before the replacement can be made.
The Municipal Accountant take up the necessary adjusting entries to record the stale checks and reflect the changes in the financial statements.

We recommended the following:
Prospectively, the Municipality strictly observe the guidelines on the receipts, utilization and reporting BGCM for the easy monitoring, and fully adhere with the rules to attain a unified, cohesive and orderly implementation of national policy to address an emergency.
The Management prioritize in setting up of their LGU official website.  Prior to its operation, the Municipality should consider to formally notify the concerned oversight agencies of the non-posting of the Reports on the Utilization of the Bayanihan Grant and the reason thereof.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of drugs and medicines held for distribution as part of inventory; and
Direct the Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain and update stock cards and inventory ledger cards, respectively, for each inventory item and corresponding issuances be covered by the RIS.  Likewise, the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) should be prepared as a basis in recognizing the expense portion of the inventory.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office should inform the concerned suppliers and employees of the unclaimed payables, otherwise issue a certification on the status of long outstanding obligations to facilitate the reversion of funds.
The Municipal Accountant recognize only the duly approved payables with complete supporting documents and review the Accounts Payable and Due to Officers and Employees account for obligations that remain unclaimed for more than two years and revert to unappropriated surplus as required by Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Management through the GAD Focal Point System fast track the establishment of sex-disaggregated data and update the GAD database to have a more complete understanding of GAD issues and concerns.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the designated GSO personnel to conduct physical inventory of its properties and render a report thereon as required under Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume 1 of the NGAS Manual.  Hence, exercise of due diligence in the conduct of physical count and preparation of inventory report is required from the General Services Office (GSO) personnel, otherwise, impose the suspension of payment of salaries pursuant to Section 122(2) of PD No. 1445 until they have complied with the requirements of the Commission

We recommended that the LCE ascertain that the progress of the inventory taking is being monitored properly.

We also recommended that the LCE require the GSO and the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) to reconcile the inventory reports with the books of accounts.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:

AMT to submit immediately the paid transactions to the MAO for recording in the books of account; and 

Municipal Accountant (MA) to monitor strictly and ensure that all paid transactions was recorded completely in the books.

We recommended that the LCE require strictly the MA to prepare without fail the required subsidiary ledgers for the account Due to NGAs as required under Section 10 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs to substantiate the details of the GL balance.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to comply with Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011.  See to it that monthly BRS and its supporting documents are regularly prepared and submitted to COA for audit.

We likewise recommended that the MA be diligent enough in the performance of his duties and functions especially in the reconciliation of the CIB balance with the bank records, otherwise, suspension of payment of salaries pursuant to Section 122(2) of PD No. 1445 shall be imposed until he shall have complied with the requirements of the aforementioned law and regulation.

Assign additional staff/s to the MAO so that BRS and other required financial reports are prepared and submitted on time.

We recommended that the LCE direct the AMT to close the depository account with PNB-Virac Branch and transfer immediately the amounts withdrawn to any authorized government banks to safeguard LGU’s funds.

We recommended that the LCE:

Require the immediate refund of the amount of ₱489,470 from persons named liable in the Notices of Disallowance to be issued by the Auditor;

Require the AMT to see to it that loan amortizations are paid on or before due dates; and

Stop payment of penalties on late payments of loans out of government funds..

We recommended that the LCE direct the ME to monitor strictly all infrastructure projects implemented by private contractors to ensure that projects were completed within the construction period as stipulated in the contracts.   

We likewise recommended that the contracts for “Installation of Wind Pump” be terminated because of failure of the contractors to deliver the projects as scheduled.  The grounds and procedures for termination of contracts are provided in Annex I of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184.  

In this connection the LGU may resort to Negotiated Procurement under “Take-over of Contracts” as provided under Section V.D.3 of Annex H of the immediately preceding IRR.   The action is without prejudice of imposing liquidated damages (LD) to the erring contractor/s to be deducted from any subsequent money claim corresponding to the 38 per cent project accomplishment.

We recommended that the LCE:

direct the BAC to furnish the Auditor with a copy of contracts/purchase orders together with supporting documents within five working days from the perfection of the contract for review and evaluation; and 

require the BAC and TWG to verify and validate thoroughly all documents and statements submitted by bidders during bid evaluation and post-qualification activities.
We recommended that the HOPE ensure that the required performance security had been posted prior to the signing of the contract.

We recommend that the LCE direct the AMT to submit immediately to the MAO all paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls, and official receipts.  Subsequently, require the MA to submit on time the recorded accounts including interim FS together with the status of appropriation, allotment and obligations, journals and other reports in conformity to the above mentioned provisions.
We recommended that the LCE direct the HRM Officer and MA to support payrolls of JO workers with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipality cause the immediate and final closure of the LGU’s dumpsite by following-up incessantly the approval of the SCRP and securing the ATC from the DENR RO V.  Such immediate action is necessary to address if not prevent, detrimental ecological issues and their effects on human health and to the environment.

Accordingly, we also recommended that Management furnish immediately the Auditor with the approved closure plan and ATC pertaining to the dumpsite at Barangay Oga for audit purposes.
We recommended that the LGU adhere strictly to Section V of COA Circular No. 2014 - 001 for timely submission of GAD reports.

We commended the BAC for a job well done.  We also recommended that the Committee observe continuously the timelines on the procurement activities prescribed in the Annex C of revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in the conduct of biddings.

We recommended that the LCE:

require the MPDO to monitor closely the PPAs funded out of the 20% DF in accordance with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; and

direct the ME and other project proponents to implement immediately all unimplemented programs/projects/activities (PPA) including those in the pipeline as embodied in the approved Annual Investment Plan in order to optimize the delivery of basic services to beneficiaries.

We recommended that the LDRRMO monitor and evaluate regularly the status of implementation of the LDRRMP in compliance with Section 11 of RA 10121. Ensure that PPAs which need to be undertaken in advance are given priority in the implementation of projects to avoid, lessen or limit the potential adverse impact of disasters. 

Specifically, we recommended the following measures:

the MDRRM Officer to prepare and submit to the GSO all Purchase Requests  for all projects chargeable to the 5% LDRRMF in the first quarter of each year to prevent delay on procurement process.  In turn, the GSO,  Budget Office, Mayor’s Office and BAC facilitate the procurement process; and

the MDRRM Officer to plan properly all PPAs funded from 70% Mitigation Fund to make these feasible for implementation during the year to anticipate, respond to and recover effectively from the impacts of likely, imminent and current hazardous events or conditions.

We recommended that the LSB monitor strictly the implementation of PPAs prioritized in the budget to ensure utilization of the SEF.  We suggested that the MBO should inform periodically the LSB of the status of implementation of projects chargeable to SEF (including usage of funds) to maximize the utilization thereof.

As for the unrecorded and unliquidated CAs, we recommended that the LCE direct the:

AMT to settle immediately the said CA in accordance with Section 1 of  Circular No. 2012-004; and

MA to monitor strictly and ensure that all paid transactions are recorded completely in the books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the designated GSO personnel to conduct physical inventory of its properties and render a report thereon as required under Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume 1 of the NGAS Manual.  Hence, exercise of due diligence in the conduct of physical count and preparation of inventory report is required from the GSO personnel, otherwise, impose the suspension of payment of salaries pursuant to Section 122(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 until they have complied with the requirements of the Commission.

We recommended that the LCE ascertain that the progress of the inventory taking is being monitored properly.

We recommended that the LCE direct the offices concerned in the implementation of programs / projects to submit on time copies of MOA or any other contracts relative to fund transfers from the national agencies so that utilization of funds and the proper disposition of unexpended excess fund transfers after the completion projects could be evaluated properly.

We recommended that a resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan be passed to authorized the LCE to employ such Job order employees  in compliance with Section 77 of RA 7160,  Also, we recommended that the LCE direct the HRM Officer to support payrolls for wages with complete documentation . 

In addition, we recommended that Management prioritize the programs, projects, and activities to be done.  Only those Offices that actually need job order workers shall hire assistants in order to avoid unnecessary government expenses.  The LCE shall monitor and control strictly the hiring of Job Order workers performing utility, clerical and administrative functions based on necessity.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately to the Accounting Office all paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls, and official receipts.   Subsequently, require the Municipal Accountant to submit on time the recorded accounts including interim financial statements together with the status of appropriation, allotment and obligations, journals and other reports in conformity with the above mentioned provisions.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to:

 Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections; and

Prepare and submit regularly a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the Sanggunian for the body to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection including enforcing levy on real property as imposed by Sections 256 and 258 of RA 7160.

We recommended that Management secure legal documents as proof of ownership of the land where various permanent structures were built. We also recommended that the LCE ensure that ownership of the land is legally transferred to the LGU to avoid future legal actions from rightful owners.

We recommended that the LCE should require the MT to deposit intact the full amount she received or collected to the authorized depository bank and refrain from retaining part of her collections in her custody.

We recommended that the MA exercise diligence in discharging his duties and functions and be more cautious in recording transactions to avoid misrepresentation of the accounts in the FS.

We also recommended that adjustments be made in the books of accounts to correct the balances of the affected accounts in the FS.
Appropriate sanction(s) is recommended against the erring MT for acts she committed in violation of Section 4(2) of PD No. 1445.
We recommended that Management comply with all the provisions stipulated in the MOA.

We recommended that the LGU’s Appraisal and Disposal Committee to take the following measures in connection with the disposal, to wit:

Appraise the market value of the unserviceable properties and waste materials to be approved by COA; and

Dispose the unserviceable properties and waste materials in accordance to the most appropriate mode of disposition of property as provided in Section 79 of RA No. 7160 which are either through, a) public auction; b) sale through negotiation; c) transfer without cost to other offices or department or any government agencies; and d) by destruction.

We recommended that the LCE require the designated GSO or equivalent to prepare, submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and attach all the necessary supporting documents as required under Section 361 e1, f & g of GAAM Volume 1.
We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate 1% of the total IRA for the strengthening of the LCPC.
We recommended that the MA submit the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents amounting to ₱1.647 million for verification as to validity and propriety of the payments made by the LGU.

We recommended that the LCE should require the MA and Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe Item 9.1 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2013-3 and Section 325(a) of R.A. 7160.  We also recommended the refund of the PEI granted to the officials and employees amounting to ₱907,139.
We recommended refund of the amount and that the LGU officials shall be aware of the specific provisions of the law and related regulations and assure faithful compliance thereof.

We recommended refund of the partial claim made on RATA arrears amounting to ₱63,540 shall be made on the basis that the claimant did not actually discharge the function of her position.  Not that she was prohibited from performing due to her will or own violation, but due to her incurrence of cash shortage and the reason for her relief was only to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended to the agency to strictly observe the provision under COA Circular 2007-001 particularly Section 4.5 and 5.0 on the grant and utilization of financial assistance.  We also recommended that all NGOs/POs who were granted financial assistance shall be required to submit liquidation report for proper evaluation, monitoring and recording of the transaction in the books of accounts.

Accounting entries shall be made to set up the “Due from NGOs/POs” account.  Expense shall only be recognized upon submission of Liquidation Report together with the supporting documents as evidence of disbursements.

We recommended also to the agency to create a committee that will accredit NGOs/POs soliciting financial assistance.

We recommended that the agency should review the contract of loan, send demand letters to each loan recipients and compel debtors to pay.  The co-makers should likewise be made aware of their role in this undertaking so they too can be an instrument for collection.

We recommended the following:

The Inventory Committee conduct physical count of the Municipality’s PPE annually and prepare the RPCPPE timely and comprehensively. Furnish the Report to the Municipal Accountant and the Auditor.

The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant maintain the prescribed property cards and PPE ledger cards (PPELCs) respectively, and reconcile it with the RPCPPE.


As proof of reconciliation, the Municipal Accountant prepare at year-end a schedule/working paper showing that each and all of the PPE items per books were reconciled against the RPCPPE.

We recommended the following:

Accountable forms be kept in storage spaces with adequate physical controls and secured from humidity and other weather conditions, such as in locked metal cabinets.

The Acting Municipal Treasurer or Officer-In-Charge in her absence closely monitor that receipt and issuance of accountable forms are properly and completely recorded in the record book in the required form, and its release be duly acknowledged by the accountable officers with their printed names and signatures.

All accountable officers prepare the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) timely for consolidation and monitoring. 

Additional accountable forms not be issued to accountable officers who have not remitted their prior collections.

The Municipal Treasurer prepare a list of obsolete and spoiled accountable forms and request inspection from the COA for proper disposal thereof.

We recommended that Management comply with the Show Cause Order of the PENRO and exert effort in seeing to it that the infrastructures already constructed will not be put to waste. Prospectively, all projects of the Municipality be implemented with consideration to existing environmental laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended the following:

Management strictly enforce and monitor the completion of the new Pandan Municipal Hall, at its earliest possible date, to avoid additional expenditures on rentals of office spaces. 

To facilitate the timely transfer of offices, procurement activities for the purchase of furniture and fixture and air conditioning system to complement the building be likewise timely to coincide with the completion date of the new Pandan Municipal Hall. 

The Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant compute for and deduct liquidated damages from payments due the contractor because the project was not finished within its intended completion date.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive cause the immediate liquidation of long outstanding cash advances for local travels of officers and employees. Salaries and emoluments of officers and employees, or terminal leave benefits of former officials, be immediately withheld and same be applied in satisfaction of their unliquidated cash advances.

No additional cash advances to officers and employees be granted unless prior cash advances are completely and fully liquidated. Partial liquidation of cash advances for local travels be avoided.

Strictly enforce the reglementary period on the liquidation of cash advances for local travels which is within 30 days after return to permanent official station and not only when the need for another cash advance for local travel arise.

We recommended that responsible officials immediately submit accounts and reports to the Office of the Auditor to facilitate timely conduct of audit. Observe strictly the provisions of P.D. 1445, MNGAS for LGUs and pertinent COA circulars on the submission of accounts and reports.

We recommended the following:

Management take charge and hasten the operation of the CFLC.


Coordinate with BFAR-6 and lot owner to clarify the roles of each of the stakeholders on the operation of the CFLC.


Immediately resolve issues on turn-over and provision of right-of-way so that the project can operate according to its purpose.

We recommended the following:

Consider the creation of a separate Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office and the appointment of a full-time MENRO who will implement the SWM program of the Municipality. For the meantime, only permanent personnel of the Municipality be designated to supervise the SWM program.

Because the 10-year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan was already approved, the Municipality request for and access the National Solid Waste Management Fund amounting to ₱1.500 million to implement additional SWM projects and activities.

Create a Local Solid Waste Management Fund to which funds for the SWM program, which include the Municipality’s own appropriations, collections of SWM fees and fines, grants and donations, shares from public-private partnerships and incentives, be pooled. 

On the closure of the Municipality’s final disposal facility, provide for another area immediately and with urgency to where non-recyclable/non-recoverable materials and special wastes be disposed.

Coordinate with other local government units and the private sector in the establishment of a compliant sanitary landfill facility.

We recommended that all projects, programs, and activities implemented by the Municipality, whether locally funded or as an implementing agency, not bear the name, picture, image, motto, logo, color motif, initials or other symbol or graphic representation of any government official. Strict compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-003 and COA Circular No. 2013-004 on “unnecessary” expenditures is enjoined.

We reiterate the following recommendations:

Re-organize the Municipality’s GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to be composed of an Executive Committee (Execom), a Technical Working Group (TWG) and/or a Secretariat. Membership for each of the groups under the GFPS to include individuals from different sectors and various offices/departments of the Municipality.

The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01. 

Administer the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) tool to attribute regular programs and projects of the Municipality to the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB).

The GFPS make representation with and assist the Sangguniang Bayan in the formulation of the Municipality’s GAD Code or ordinance.

We recommended the following:

a.	Require the Binirayan Foundation, Inc. to immediately submit its FUR because the time to liquidate has already elapsed.

We recommended the following:

b. If found to have manufacturing defects, avail of the warranty as provided in RA No. 9184. On the other hand, in case of abuse/misuse by the end-users, hold the users concerned liable.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly enforce and monitor compliance with the accounting and reporting requirements for the LDRRMF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002. The MDRRMO coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in the timely preparation and submission of the monthly Report and Utilization of LDRRMF.

We recommended the following:

The Acting Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to collect delinquent real property taxes. Avail of all collection remedies applicable in the current circumstance of the Municipality.



The Acting Municipal Treasurer issue warrants of levy and conduct public sale or auction of delinquent real properties. These are indispensable actions to avoid the criminal and administrative penalties provided in RA No. 7160. Coordinate with other local treasurers who had recently conducted public sale or auction in their own local government units to adopt their procedures and best practices.

Management provide support to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in the conduct of public sale or auction. Include in the Municipality’s appropriations the budgetary requirements of the activity and provide augmentation of personnel when needed.

We recommended the following:

Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account (Account Number 5-02-99-990) be used only to record expenses not falling under any of the specific maintenance and other operating expense accounts.

We recommended that Management prioritize processing of transfer of land titles in the name of the Municipality. To secure ownership on succeeding purchase of lots, payment be made only after the title has been transferred in the name of the Municipality.

We recommended the following:

Maximize utilization of the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF for disaster-related PAPs.

Include PAPs to be funded out of the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF in the LDRRMFIP of the Municipality. Synchronize and consolidate planning for and utilization of the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF with that of the current year LDRRMF. Prepare timely the LDRRMFIP, and the form be in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 and incorporated in the Municipality’s AIP.

Re-program and/or update PAPs to be funded out of the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF every year to reflect the current DRRM needs of the Municipality.

We commended the Municipality for effectively implementing the project on prohibition of plastic bags/pouches/cups usage under the solid waste management program. We reiterated our recommendation that separate appropriations for the MENRO be provided and a full-time MENR Officer, either a Department Head or OIC meantime that finances do not permit, be appointed to carry out all other environmental programs.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management create a separate Municipal Tourism Office with its own appropriations. Due to the magnitude of work and responsibility, consider upgrade of position and/or designation of the Tourism Operations Officer I and provide the Office with at least another permanent personnel. In support of the Resort as a feasible economic enterprise, collections derived therefrom be utilized only for tourism investments, projects and activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer or with the concerned Department Head timely inform the Auditor through a letter (See Appendix C.2 – Pro-forma Letter) that the appropriate signboard is already posted after award of an infrastructure project or before the start of a program/activity. Strict compliance to the prohibition on the display/affixture of items related to government officials on signboards and on maintaining and updating the project status portion of the signboards as provided in COA Circular No. 2013-004 as amended by COA Circulars No. 2015-006 and 2016-003 likewise be observed.

We recommended that management submit the duly filled out Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation Form on the audit observations and recommendations embodied in the CY 2016 AAR to the Office of the Auditor. All items in the form be accomplished including the detailed action plan, person/department responsible, target implementation date, and status of implementation.

We recommended the following:

a.	The Municipality provide a separate appropriation for MCPC programs, activities and projects (PAPs) equivalent to one percent of its share from the IRA. Only PAPs enumerated in DILG Memorandum No. 2012-120 be charged out of the separate appropriation.

c.	PAPs on children be complete and inclusive, and to contain programs to assist children in need of special protection (CNSP) and intervention and diversion programs for children in conflict with the law (CICL) among others.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record all valid reconciling items that require adjustments and corrections in the books. Identify checks which became stale and prepare adjusting entries to restore the amounts to the Cash in Bank account. Likewise, make representation regarding items that need adjustments and corrections by the depository bank. Support all reconciling items appearing in the BRSs with complete documents and attach copies of monthly bank statements to BRSs submitted to the Auditor.

We recommended the following: 

Provide the budgetary requirements for personnel services, maintenance and operating expenditures, and capital outlay of the MDRRMO which shall be sourced from the General Fund to effectively carry out DRRM projects and activities of the Municipality. 

Prioritize creation and appointment of three statutory DRRM staff who will assist the Municipal DRRM Officer in administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning.

We recommended the following:

Organize the Municipal Tourism Council and Tourism Planning Committee, and in coordination with the Municipal Tourism Officer, prepare a comprehensive Local Tourism Development Plan and facilitate its submission to the Sangguniang Bayan for adoption. Utilize the DOT-DILG-DENR-DAP Tourism Guidebook for Local Government Units.
Mobilize the concerned offices in the early planning and implementation of development projects to avoid incurring possible additional costs due to delayed project implementation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the designated GSO personnel to conduct physical inventory of its properties and render a report thereon as required under Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume 1 of the NGAS Manual.  Hence, exercise of due diligence in the conduct of physical count and preparation of inventory report is required from the General Services Office (GSO) personnel, otherwise, impose the suspension of payment of salaries pursuant to Section 122(2) of PD No. 1445 until they have complied with the requirements of the Commission

We recommended that the LCE ascertain that the progress of the inventory taking is being monitored properly.

We also recommended that the LCE require the GSO and the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) to reconcile the inventory reports with the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

The Inventory Committee conduct physical count of the Municipality’s PPE annually and prepare the RPCPPE timely and comprehensively. Furnish the Report to the Municipal Accountant and the Auditor.
 

The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant maintain the prescribed property cards and PPE ledger cards (PPELCs) respectively, and reconcile it with the RPCPPE.

We recommended the following:

The collecting officers prepare their cashbooks correctly by recording all their transactions daily, and closing, ruling and certifying their accounts and transactions in the cashbook at the end of each month.


The designated liquidating officer strictly verify the Reports of Collection and Deposit (RCDs) prepared by the collecting officers, especially on the correctness of the beginning and ending balances of their cash accountabilities and the completeness of the recording of their accountabilities on accountable forms. The collecting function of the designated liquidating officer be transferred to another accountable officer for him to properly discharge his responsibilities.

The Acting Municipal Treasurer require all accountable officers to prepare the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) for consolidation and monitoring. Accountable forms not be issued to accountable officers who have not remitted their prior collections.

The Municipal Mayor relieve the accountable officers who defaulted in the past from their duties as collecting officers of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. 

The Municipal Mayor designate other permanent employees of the Municipality as collecting officers and be properly bonded to assist the Municipal Treasurer’s Office in its collecting function. Cash collections not be assigned to job order personnel.

We recommended the following:

a.	All grants and fund transfers by the Municipality to NGOs/POs be in consonance with COA Circular No. 2007-001. Entitlement of funds by NGOs/POs be supported with required documents and the MOA shall contain the required terms of reference.

b.	Submit justification why Binirayan Foundation, Inc. was granted funds despite the absence of eligibility documents and why the MOA did not contain the required terms of reference.

c.	The Municipal Accountant prepare the following journal entry to correct the recording of the grant to Binirayan Foundation, Inc.:

Account Name                Code              Debit        Credit   
Due from NGOs/POs      1-03-06-030  100,000
Prior Period Adjustment  3-01-01-020	      100,000
        
To correct recording of grant to Binirayan Foundation, Inc. recorded as Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses instead of Due from NGOs/POs in CY 2018.

Reverse the journal entry above only upon liquidation through submission of the fund utilization report (FUR).

Require the Binirayan Foundation, Inc. to immediately submit its FUR because the time to liquidate has already elapsed.

We recommended the following:

a.	Explain in writing why the patrol boat was already non-functional two months after its delivery and acceptance. 

b.	If found to have manufacturing defects, avail of the warranty as provided in RA No. 9184. On the other hand, in case of abuse/misuse by the end-users, hold the users concerned liable.

We recommended the following:

Re-activate the Municipality’s GFPS, composed of an Execom, a TWG and/or a Secretariat. Each of the groups under the GFPS be composed of individuals from different sectors and various offices/departments of the Municipality.

The GFPS to actively perform its functions, roles and responsibilities in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 especially in the timely preparation and submission of the GPB.

We recommended the following:

Properly plan for and maximize access to all allowed sources of financing for solid waste management projects and activities as provided in the IRR of RA No. 9003.


Follow-up the status of approval of the 10-year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan by the National Solid Waste Management Commission. Once approved, avail the National Solid Waste Management Fund to implement additional solid waste management projects and activities not funded by the Municipality’s regular appropriations and/or other sources.

Create a Local Solid Waste Management Fund. Funds that will constitute it be sourced from the Municipality’s appropriations, collections of solid waste management fees and fines, grants and donations, shares from public-private partnerships, and incentives. The Fund be used solely for solid waste management projects and activities.

Prioritize funding for the maintenance and operation of the Municipality’s residual containment area (RCA).

We recommended the following:

The Acting Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to collect delinquent real property taxes. Avail of all collection remedies applicable in the current circumstance of the Municipality.



The Acting Municipal Treasurer issue warrants of levy and conduct public sale or auction of delinquent real properties. These are indispensable actions to avoid the criminal and administrative penalties provided in RA No. 7160. Coordinate with other local treasurers who had recently conducted public sale or auction in their own local government units to adopt their procedures and best practices.

Management provide support to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in the conduct of public sale or auction. Include in the Municipality’s appropriations the budgetary requirements of the activity and provide augmentation of personnel when needed.

We recommended that lease of office spaces by the Municipality be coursed through proper procurement process in accordance with RA No. 9184 and its 2016 revised IRR. Specific guidelines on rating lease offers according to specifications and determining reasonableness of rental rates be observed.

We recommended that technical specifications be included in the procurement of catering services and all other goods and services by the Municipality. Specifications should be detailed, contain clear description, include all requirements and consider the project’s objectives or the procurement at hand. However, in compliance with RA No. 9184, technical specifications should not include brand names and only be generic and based on relevant characteristics and/or relevant standards.

We recommended the following:

Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account (Account Number 5-02-99-990) be used only to record expenses not falling under any of the specific maintenance and other operating expense accounts.

Grants to Non-Government Organizations/People’s Organizations (NGOs/POs) for the implementation of specific projects be recorded as Due from NGOs/POs first and recorded only as expense upon liquidation (re: AOM No. 2019-006(18)-PDN dated March 12, 2019).

In the preparation of the Municipality’s budgets, avoid including programs, activities and projects in lump-sum amounts. Instead, each program, activity or project be provided with specific accounts as provided in the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.

We recommended that Management prioritize processing of transfer of land titles in the name of the Municipality. To secure ownership on succeeding purchase of lots, payment be made only after the title has been transferred in the name of the Municipality.

We recommended the following:

Maximize utilization of the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF for disaster-related PAPs.



Include PAPs to be funded out of the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF in the LDRRMFIP of the Municipality. Synchronize and consolidate planning for and utilization of the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF with that of the current year LDRRMF. Prepare timely the LDRRMFIP, and the form be in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 and incorporated in the Municipality’s AIP.
Re-program and/or update PAPs to be funded out of the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF every year to reflect the current DRRM needs of the Municipality.

We commended the Municipality for effectively implementing the project on prohibition of plastic bags/pouches/cups usage under the solid waste management program. We reiterated our recommendation that separate appropriations for the MENRO be provided and a full-time MENR Officer, either a Department Head or OIC meantime that finances do not permit, be appointed to carry out all other environmental programs.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management create a separate Municipal Tourism Office with its own appropriations. Due to the magnitude of work and responsibility, consider upgrade of position and/or designation of the Tourism Operations Officer I and provide the Office with at least another permanent personnel. In support of the Resort as a feasible economic enterprise, collections derived therefrom be utilized only for tourism investments, projects and activities.

We recommended that responsible officials immediately submit accounts and reports to the Office of the Auditor to facilitate timely conduct of audit. Provisions of PD No. 1445, pertinent COA circulars and the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs on the submission of accounts and reports be strictly observed.

We recommended that management submit the duly filled out Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation Form on the audit observations and recommendations embodied in the CY 2016 AAR to the Office of the Auditor. All items in the form be accomplished including the detailed action plan, person/department responsible, target implementation date, and status of implementation.

We recommended the following:

a.	The Municipality provide a separate appropriation for MCPC programs, activities and projects (PAPs) equivalent to one percent of its share from the IRA. Only PAPs enumerated in DILG Memorandum No. 2012-120 be charged out of the separate appropriation.

The MCPC be more active in planning for, implementing and monitoring PAPs on children. The Secretariat perform administrative work and prepare reports for the Council.

c.	PAPs on children be complete and inclusive, and to contain programs to assist children in need of special protection (CNSP) and intervention and diversion programs for children in conflict with the law (CICL) among others.
We recommended that management prepare and submit for review and endorsement the GPB timely and in accordance with the schedule set in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2016-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record all valid reconciling items that require adjustments and corrections in the books. Identify checks which became stale and prepare adjusting entries to restore the amounts to the Cash in Bank account. Likewise, make representation regarding items that need adjustments and corrections by the depository bank. Support all reconciling items appearing in the BRSs with complete documents and attach copies of monthly bank statements to BRSs submitted to the Auditor.

We recommended that the BAC procure POL of the Municipality strictly in accordance with RA No. 9184 and its revised IRR. Specifically, we recommended the following:

Alternative methods of procurement be resorted to in the procurement of POL only when justified by circumstances specifically provided in RA No. 9184, its revised IRR, and GPPB issuances.

Technical specifications of POL products to be procured be itemized and detailed in invitations to bid/requests for quotations. Other terms and requirements for POL procurement, which lies within the prerogative and sound discretion of the procuring entity, be included. These specifications, terms and requirements be likewise reflected in the purchase orders/contracts of agreement.

We recommended that services of individual consultants be procured in accordance with RA No. 9184 and its revised IRR, the BAC to undertake negotiation with, and validate the capability of the individual consultants to undertake and fulfill the consultancy work based on the Terms of Reference, and to recommend to the Municipal Mayor the award of the contract. In accordance with Section 54.6(e) of the 2016 revised IRR of RA No. 9184, individual consultants are mandated to register with PhilGEPS and provide a PhilGEPS registration number as a condition for award of the contract. Accomplishment report and additional documents be submitted justifying the necessity and propriety of the claim of the individual legal consultant.

We recommended that management strictly observe the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 in the grant of cash advances for CF. Specifically:

Submit cash advance vouchers for CF and all its supporting documents to the COA ATL within seven days after release of checks for timely evaluation and audit.

Include a separate and specific POP budget in the Annual Appropriations Ordinance, which will then be the basis of computation of the allowable CF.

We recommended the following: 

Provide the budgetary requirements for personnel services, maintenance and operating expenditures, and capital outlay of the MDRRMO which shall be sourced from the General Fund to effectively carry out DRRM projects and activities of the Municipality. 

Prioritize creation and appointment of three statutory DRRM staff who will assist the Municipal DRRM Officer in administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning. 

Utilize the LDRRMF solely for DRRM-related projects and activities. Administrative expenses be charged from the separate MDDRMO budget.

We recommended the following:

Organize the Municipal Tourism Council and Tourism Planning Committee, and in coordination with the Municipal Tourism Officer, prepare a comprehensive Local Tourism Development Plan and facilitate its submission to the Sangguniang Bayan for adoption. Utilize the DOT-DILG-DENR-DAP Tourism Guidebook for Local Government Units.

Prioritize inclusion of PAPs that support tourism regulation, sustainability in tourism and mitigation of negative impacts of tourism in the Local Tourism Development Plan which should also be integrated with the other plans of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Accountant monitor the refund of the said cash advance granted to the Municipal Treasurer. Otherwise, institute measures as mentioned in Sections 7 and 8 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.
Mobilize the concerned offices in the early planning and implementation of development projects to avoid incurring possible additional costs due to delayed project implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances. 



The Municipal Accountant diligently monitor and enforce settlement thereof and if necessary, management deduct the amount of unliquidated cash advances from their claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned department heads evaluate and assess the capability of the Barangays to fully implement the LGU projects before transferring the funds. Monitoring of the fund utilization by the Barangays should be conducted regularly by the Municipal Government so that the target completion date as agreed shall be met and ensure that the transferred funds are utilized for its intended purpose.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the designated GSO personnel to conduct physical inventory of its properties and render a report thereon as required under Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume 1 of the NGAS Manual. Ascertain that the progress of the inventory taking is being monitored by the LCE.
	
We likewise recommended that the personnel concerned of the GSO be required to exercise diligence in the performance of their duties and functions especially on the physical count and preparation of inventory report, otherwise, impose the suspension of payment of salaries pursuant to Section 122(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 until they have complied with the requirements of the Commission.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  (LCE)  direct  the
designated GSO personnel to conduct physical  inventory  of  its  properties
and render a report thereon  as  required  under  Section  124,  Chapter  7,
Volume 1 of the NGAS Manual.   Hence,  exercise  of  due  diligence  in  the
conduct of physical count and preparation of inventory  report  is  required
from the GSO personnel, otherwise,  impose  the  suspension  of  payment  of
salaries pursuant to Section 122(2) of Presidential Decree  No.  1445  until
they have complied with the requirements of the Commission.

We recommended that  the  LCE  ascertain  that  the  progress  of  the
inventory taking is being monitored properly.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to comply
with Section 74 of PD No. 1445  and  Section  3.2  of  Commission  on  Audit
Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.  See to it that monthly  BRS  and
its supporting documents are regularly prepared and  submitted  to  COA  for
audit.


We likewise recommended that  the  Municipal  Accountant  be  diligent
enough in the performance of his duties  and  functions  especially  in  the
reconciliation  of  the  Cash  in  Bank  balance  with  the  bank   records,
otherwise, suspension of payment of salaries pursuant to Section  122(2)  of
Presidential Decree No. 1445 shall be imposed until he shall  have  complied
with the requirements of the Commission.


Assign additional staff/s to accounting office in order that  BRS  and
other required financial reports are prepared and submitted on time.

We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  prepare  the  necessary
adjusting entries in  order  to  recognize  the  appropriate  PPE  accounts.
Henceforth, all capital expenditures shall be recognized first  as  PPE  and
recorded under appropriate PPE accounts in all phases of construction.

We recommended that the  LCE  direct  the  offices  concerned  in  the
implementation of programs/projects to submit on time copies of MOA or any
other contracts relative  to  fund  transfers  from  the  national  government
agencies so  that  utilization  of  funds  and  the  proper  disposition  of
unexpended excess fund transfers after  the  completion  projects  could  be
evaluated properly.

We recommended that the  LCE  instruct  the  Municipal  Treasurer  and
Municipal Accountant to transfer immediately the unexpended balance  of  the
LDRRMF for CYs 2015 -2018 from General Fund to Trust Fund  as  appearing  in
the Fund Utilization Report for the same years by issuing  a  check  payable
to trust fund and by preparing the corresponding adjusting  entries  on  the
fund transfer.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to:


    1.  Submit all missing disbursement vouchers to the accounting  office
       for proper recording in the books of the LGU;


    2. Prepare the RCI containing all checks issued during the period  and
       submit this to the Accounting Office  together  with  corresponding
       DVs;


    3. Ensure that complete supporting documents are attached to  the  DVs
       before the paid transactions  are  transmitted  to  the  accounting
       office; and


    4. Observe the 90 day period within which to submit  the  missing  DVs
       before suspensions mature into disallowance.

     We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare  and  record
JEV upon receipt of DVs and  supporting  documents  to  adjust  and  correct
balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Accountant  present  the  current
portion  of  the  loans  payable  amounting  to  ₱3.92  million  to  current
liability in the Statement of Financial Position.   It should  be  disclosed
in the financial statement as the amount due  to  be  settled  after  twelve
months from the reporting date to present fairly the financial statement  of
the agency and in accordance with paragraph 70, 71 and 80 (  c  )  of  PPSAS
Volume 1.

We recommended that a resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan be  passed
to authorized the LCE to employ such  Job  order  employees   in  compliance
with Section 77 of RA 7160,  Also, we recommended that the  LCE  direct  the
HRM Officer to support payrolls for wages with complete documentation .


In addition, we recommended that Management prioritize  the  programs,
projects, and activities to be done.  Only those Offices that actually  need
job order workers shall  hire  assistants  in  order  to  avoid  unnecessary
government expenses.  The LCE shall monitor and control strictly the  hiring
of  Job  Order  workers  performing  utility,  clerical  and  administrative
functions based on necessity.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer  to  submit
immediately  to  the  Accounting  Office  all  paid  disbursement  vouchers,
payrolls, and  official  receipts.    Subsequently,  require  the  Municipal
Accountant to  submit  on  time  the  recorded  accounts  including  interim
financial statements together with the status  of  appropriation,  allotment
and obligations, journals and other reports in  conformity  with  the  above
mentioned provisions.

We recommended that the LCE direct  the  Municipal  Engineer  and  the
Municipal  Planning  and  Development  Coordinator  (MPDC)  to  provide  the
Auditors with a list  of  all  on-going  PPAs  and  those  that  are  to  be
implemented during the year.  Likewise,  the  MPDC  and  Municipal  Engineer
shall inform  the  Auditors  that  appropriate  projects  signboards  and/or
public notices are already posted.

We recommended  that  the  LCE  require  the  Municipal  Planning  and
Development Officer to give equal priority to  the  implementation  of
projects funded from different funds, including from 20% DF.    This  is  to
promote/encourage an optimum and efficient utilization of all funds  and
achieve the desired socio-economic development and  environmental  outcomes.
We recommended that proper and efficient  planning  of  activities  be  done
to  ensure  that  all  targeted  projects  and  activities  are  implemented
as scheduled so that intended beneficiaries can enjoy  the benefits that
could be derived from the projects once completed.


We likewise recommended the hiring of additional staffs in the Offices
of the Municipal Planning and Development and Municipal Engineer.

We recommended that the LDRRMC  monitor  and  evaluate  regularly  the
status of implementation of the LDRRMP in compliance with Section 11  of  RA
10121.  Ensure that PPAs that need to be undertaken  in  advance  are  given
priority in the implementation of projects to avoid,  lessen  or  limit  the
potential adverse impact of disasters.


Specifically, we recommended the following measures:


       1. the MDRRM Officer to prepare and submit to the General  Services
          Office all Purchase Requests for all projects chargeable to  the
          5% LDRRMF in the first quarter of each year to prevent delay  on
          procurement process.   In turn, the GSO,  Budget Office, Mayor’s
          Office and Bids and Awards Committee facilitate the  procurement
          process; and


       2. the MDRRM Officer to plan properly  all  PPAs  funded  from  70%
          Mitigation Fund to make these feasible for implementation during
          the year to anticipate, respond to and recover effectively  from
          the impacts of likely, imminent and current hazardous events  or
          conditions.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to:


    1.  Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on  the  updated  data  and
       information of real Property Taxes  for  proper  implementation  of
       prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections; and


    2. Prepare and submit regularly a certified list of all real  property
       tax delinquencies to the Sanggunian for the body to assist  in  the
       enforcement of the remedies for collection including enforcing levy
       on real property as imposed by Sections 256 and 258 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA  and  MAO  to  ensure  that
complete documentations are attached to disbursement vouchers for  transfers
of funds to proponent group.

We recommended that the LCE require the:


    1. Municipal Accountant to strictly  comply  with  the  provisions  of
       Section 5 of Revenue Regulations No. 17-2003 dated March  31,  2003
       regarding remittance of taxes withheld at source; and


    2. Personnel in charge in the preparation  of  monthly  remittance  of
       taxes to ensure  that  the  amount  remitted  is  the  same  amount
       withheld.

We recommended the following:
The Inventory Committee conduct physical count of the Municipality’s PPE annually and prepare the RPCPPE timely and comprehensively. Furnish the Report to the Municipal Accountant and the Auditor.
The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant maintain the prescribed property cards and PPE ledger cards (PPELCs) respectively, and reconcile it with the RPCPPE.
As proof of reconciliation, the Municipal Accountant prepare at year-end a schedule/working paper showing that each and all of the PPE items per books were reconciled against the RPCPPE.

We recommended the following:
The collecting officers prepare their cashbooks correctly by recording all their transactions daily, and closing, ruling and certifying their accounts and transactions in the cashbook at the end of each month.
The designated liquidating officer strictly verify the Reports of Collection and Deposit (RCDs) prepared by the collecting officers, especially on the correctness of the beginning and ending balances of their cash accountabilities and the completeness of the recording of their accountabilities on accountable forms. The collecting function of the designated liquidating officer be transferred to another accountable officer for him to properly discharge his responsibilities.
The Acting Municipal Treasurer require all accountable officers to prepare the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) for consolidation and monitoring. Accountable forms not be issued to accountable officers who have not remitted their prior collections.
The Municipal Mayor relieve the accountable officers who defaulted in the past from their duties as collecting officers of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.
The Municipal Mayor designate other permanent employees of the Municipality as collecting officers and be properly bonded to assist the Municipal Treasurer’s Office in its collecting function. Cash collections not be assigned to job order personnel.

We recommended the following:
All grants and fund transfers by the Municipality to NGOs/POs be in consonance with COA Circular No. 2007-001. Entitlement of funds by NGOs/POs be supported with required documents and the MOA shall contain the required terms of reference.
Submit justification why Binirayan Foundation, Inc. was granted funds despite the absence of eligibility documents and why the MOA did not contain the required terms of reference.
The Municipal Accountant prepare the following journal entry to correct the recording of the grant to Binirayan Foundation, Inc.:
Account Name		Code			Debit		Credit
Due from NGOs/POs	       1-03-06-030	       100,000.00
Prior Period Adjustment         3-01-01-020					100,000.00
To correct recording of grant to Binirayan Foundation, Inc. recorded as Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses instead of Due from NGOs/POs in CY 2018.
Reverse the journal entry above only upon liquidation through submission of the fund utilization report (FUR).
Require the Binirayan Foundation, Inc. to immediately submit its FUR because the time to liquidate has already elapsed.

We recommended the following:
Explain in writing why the patrol boat was already non-functional two months after its delivery and acceptance.
If found to have manufacturing defects, avail of the warranty as provided in RA No. 9184. On the other hand, in case of abuse/misuse by the end-users, hold the users concerned liable.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly enforce and monitor compliance with the accounting and reporting requirements for the LDRRMF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002. The MDRRMO coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in the timely preparation and submission of the monthly Report and Utilization of LDRRMF.

We recommended the following:
Re-activate the Municipality’s GFPS, composed of an Execom, a TWG and/or a Secretariat. Each of the groups under the GFPS be composed of individuals from different sectors and various offices/departments of the Municipality.
The GFPS to actively perform its functions, roles and responsibilities in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 especially in the timely preparation and submission of the GPB.

We recommended the following:
Properly plan for and maximize access to all allowed sources of financing for solid waste management projects and activities as provided in the IRR of RA No. 9003.
Follow-up the status of approval of the 10-year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan by the National Solid Waste Management Commission. Once approved, avail the National Solid Waste Management Fund to implement additional solid waste management projects and activities not funded by the Municipality’s regular appropriations and/or other sources.
Create a Local Solid Waste Management Fund. Funds that will constitute it be sourced from the Municipality’s appropriations, collections of solid waste management fees and fines, grants and donations, shares from public-private partnerships, and incentives. The Fund be used solely for solid waste management projects and activities.
Prioritize funding for the maintenance and operation of the Municipality’s residual containment area (RCA).

We recommended the following:
The Acting Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to collect delinquent real property taxes. Avail of all collection remedies applicable in the current circumstance of the Municipality.
The Acting Municipal Treasurer issue warrants of levy and conduct public sale or auction of delinquent real properties. These are indispensable actions to avoid the criminal and administrative penalties provided in RA No. 7160. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management establish strong cash management mechanisms and controls through the following:

a. 	The Acting Municipal Treasurer require all accountable officers to maintain a cashbook and to record all their transactions daily, and to close, rule, and certify their accounts and transactions in the cashbook at the end of each month.



b. 	The Acting Municipal Treasurer require the accountable officers to submit their Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) daily to the Cashier, and the Cashier to strictly verify the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) prepared by the accountable officers, especially on the correctness of the beginning and ending balances of their cash accountabilities and the completeness of the recording of their accountabilities on accountable forms.

c. 	The Acting Municipal Treasurer require the accountable officers to prepare the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) for consolidation and monitoring, and to firmly impose remittance of collections out of the issued accountable forms within the day.

d. 	The accountable officers produce immediately the missing funds.



e. 	The Municipal Mayor immediately relieve the defaulting accountable officers with cash shortages from their duties as Collecting Officers of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. Their relief shall be without prejudice to the continuation and completion of our examination, submission of our report, and any other future action.

Reiterated in this year’s AAR, Obs. No. 2.

Fully implemented

Organize the Municipal Tourism Council and Tourism Planning Committee, and in coordination with the Municipal Tourism Officer, prepare a comprehensive Local Tourism Development Plan and facilitate its submission to the Sangguniang Bayan for adoption.
